history.text: The Kingdom of Urundi emerged, giving the area a defined monarchical state structure.
The territory became part of German East Africa, shaping its later political and regional identity.
The territory became part of Ruanda-Urundi, shaping its later political and regional identity.
Burundi became independent from Belgium, marking a key step in modern statehood.
Burundi became a republic, replacing the previous constitutional arrangement.
Burundian Civil War marked a violent internal conflict that shaped Burundi's modern politics.
The current constitution came into force, setting the basic framework for today's Burundi.
